title: elsholtzia argyi var. nipponica
intro: Elsholtzia argyi var. nipponica, also known as Japanese mint, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to the presence of essential oils such as limonene and beta-pinene. The health benefits of Elsholtzia argyi var. nipponica include reducing fever and relieving headaches, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant are typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be monitored due to potential interactions with medications and allergies.
